channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Former President Bill Clinton returns to Oklahoma City 30 years after the bombing
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — Thirty years after the deadliest homegrown attack in U.S. history, former President Bill Clinton returned to Oklahoma City on Saturday to remember the people who were killed and comfort those affected by the bombing.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 250 years after America went to war for independence, a divided nation battles over its legacy
item.description: LEXINGTON, Mass. (AP) — Thousands of people came to Lexington, Massachusetts, just before dawn on Saturday to witness a reenactment of how the American Revolution began 250 years ago, with the blast of gunshot and a trail of colonial spin.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Believers say microdosing psychedelics helps them. Scientists are trying to measure the claims
item.description: Microdosing is gaining popularity with a new breed of health seekers. These self-experimenters take a very small amount of psilocybin mushrooms or LSD to try to reduce anxiety, stress and depression.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Putin announces an Easter ceasefire as Russia and Ukraine swap hundreds of POWs
item.description: Russian President Vladimir Putin announced a temporary Easter ceasefire in Ukraine starting Saturday, citing humanitarian reasons, as Russia and Ukraine swapped hundreds of captured soldiers in the largest exchange since Moscow's full-scale invasion started over three years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Florida man busted on Amtrak train with ‘small arsenal’ of weapons: AG
item.description: Jeffrey Kennerk of Florida has been indicted on more than a dozen charges related to what authorities say was a "small arsenal" of weapons he had brought onto an Amtrak train.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Members of burned LA church bear a cross through their devastated neighborhood to mark Good Friday
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Carrying a tall wooden cross on his shoulder, Pastor John Shaver walked down the street where his church had stood, just three months ago.
item.dc:creator: DEEPA BHARATH, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A South Carolina college could close after 179 years if it can't find $6 million by Tuesday
item.description: After nearly 200 years as an institution of higher education, Limestone College will soon close its doors.
item.dc:creator: Julia Keener, KRON4

item.title: Questions emerge about how a deputy's stepson became the accused gunman in deadly FSU shooting
item.description: TALLAHASSEE, Fla. (AP) — Amid the abandoned chemistry notes and other debris left behind after a deadly shooting at Florida State University are lingering questions about how the stepson of a beloved sheriff’s deputy tasked with school safety at a middle school became the accused gunman.
item.dc:creator: KATE PAYNE and HEATHER HOLLINGSWORTH, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Takeaways from AP’s report on pardoned Jan. 6 rioters being embraced in Republican politics
item.description: President Donald Trump pardoned about 1,500 Capitol rioters on his first day in office, and now some of those involved in the Jan. 6 attack are getting a platform to tell their version of events.
item.dc:creator: Ali Swenson, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: America's housing shortage by the numbers
item.description: The U.S. has been short of the homes it needs to meet its growing population for more than a decade, driving up prices and shooting down the dreams of millions of would-be buyers.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A prince and knight fall in love and the Supreme Court is asked to intervene
item.description: Some parents in a suburban Maryland county want to pull their children from elementary school classes that use books featuring LGBTQ characters.
item.dc:creator: Mark Sherman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Spring allergies are back. Here's how to check pollen levels and keep from sneezing
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Allergy season can be miserable for tens of millions of Americans when trees, grass, and other pollens cause runny noses, itchy eyes, coughing and sneezing.
item.dc:creator: KENYA HUNTER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Aftershocks and lack of resources hinder recovery work 3 weeks after Myanmar's deadly earthquake
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Basic services have yet to be restored to the areas of Myanmar worst hit by a huge earthquake three weeks ago, and emergency workers recovering bodies and clearing debris are contending with regular aftershocks and lack of resources, humanitarian services say.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Supreme Court blocks, for now, new deportations under 18th century wartime law
item.description: The Supreme Court on Saturday blocked, for now, the deportations of any Venezuelans held in northern Texas under an 18th century wartime law.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and MARK SHERMAN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration makes major cuts to Native American boarding school research projects
item.description: At least $1.6 million in federal funds for projects meant to capture and digitize stories of the systemic abuse of generations of Indigenous children in boarding schools at the hands of the U.S. government have been slashed due to federal funding cuts under President Donald Trump’s administration.
item.dc:creator: HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump’s first quarter approval rating sits at 45 percent: Poll
item.description: Less than half of U.S. adults say they have confidence in the president to recommend or do the right thing for the economy, according to the Gallup poll.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Appeals court halts Boasberg’s contempt proceedings against Trump administration
item.description: A divided federal appeals court panel on Friday temporarily halted U.S. District Judge James Boasberg’s contempt proceedings against the Trump administration over its deportation flights to El Salvador last month. The U.S. Court of Appeals for the D.C.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, NewsNation

item.title: Judge declines to further enforce order restoring AP access to White House
item.description: A federal judge on Friday declined to further enforce his order directing the White House to restore The Associated Press’s access to certain spaces, signaling that while top officials are owed a presumption of good faith, violations of his directive would be met with a heavy hand. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Inside the 'rape club prison': Former inmates tell their stories
item.description: They tell NewsNation they were assaulted by officers who worked there, and when they tried to report the abuse, they were silenced
item.dc:creator: Natasha Zouves, NewsNation

item.title: Video shows 'Call on Jesus' bus explode in ball of flames along Louisiana road
item.description: Authorities are investigating after a commuter bus wrapped with the message "Call on Jesus" caught fire Wednesday, sparking a massive explosion in North Shreveport, Louisiana.
item.dc:creator: Isabella Cheng, WGN 9

item.title: Ten Commandments, ‘In God We Trust’ in classrooms is now Arkansas law
item.description: Arkansas Gov. Sarah Huckabee Sanders signed a bill into law this week making religious displays in classrooms state law.
item.dc:creator: Alex Kienlen, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: FSU shooting: Sheriff’s office speaks on suspect’s past, deputy stepmother
item.description: The Leon County Sheriff's Office is speaking out Friday after the suspect of the Florida State University shooting was identified as the stepson of one of its deputies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 2 food service employees killed in FSU shooting
item.description: A food service worker was identified as one of two people killed when a gunman opened fire on the Florida State University campus Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Military officials identify 2 Marines killed in crash
item.description: Two Camp Pendleton Marines killed in a crash during a convoy movement in New Mexico Tuesday have been identified.
item.dc:creator: Domenick Candelieri, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ryan Gosling to star in next 'Star Wars' film, Lucasfilm announces
item.description: The Force was with many Japanese, as well as visitors from abroad, at a “Star Wars” event on Friday where Lucasfilm announced the next installation in the film franchise.
item.dc:creator: Yuri Kageyama, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump admin to cut 90% of CFPB in latest layoffs: Reports
item.description: President Donald Trump’s administration is cutting nearly 90 percent of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau’s (CFPB) employees in a push to significantly downsize the watchdog agency formed to shield U.S. consumers from financial fraud and abuse.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, KRON4

item.title: US citizen held by ICE despite judge seeing birth certificate
item.description: Juan Carlos Lopez-Gomez, who was born in the United States, was detained Wednesday in Florida by the state’s highway patrol and was charged with illegally entering the Sunshine State as an “unauthorized alien” under a state law that has been temporarily blocked by a judge, the Florida Phoenix news o
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Deer Valley Music Festival to feature Lindsey Stirling, Beach Boys, and Common
item.description: The 2025 Deer Valley Music Festival lineup has now been announced, featuring big names on the lineup — such as The Beach Boys, Lindsey Stirling, and Common.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: Train collides with funeral home limo in Tennessee; family of 6 injured
item.description: A funeral limo en route to a funeral — and carrying a family to the funeral — was struck by a train in Tennessee.
item.dc:creator: David Royer, Fox 5

item.title: Supreme Court to hear rare May arguments in birthright citizenship battle
item.description: The Supreme Court scheduled a rare May oral argument on the Trump administration’s emergency request to narrow a series of nationwide blocks on President Trump’s executive order that would restrict birthright citizenship.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Wrong man, wrong bed, wrong treatment: Patient's death prompts medical board to discipline California doctor
item.description: A California physician has been placed on five years’ probation by the state's medical board for administering a course of treatment to a patient that was intended for the patient in an adjacent hospital bed.
item.dc:creator: Robert Price, KRON4

item.title: Red Robin launches $20 Bottomless Burger Pass for National Burger Month
item.description: May is National Burger Month and Red Robin is celebrating with the launch of its Bottomless Burger Pass — a limited-time offer that gives fans a month of daily gourmet burgers and bottomless sides for just $20. The pass will be available for purchase beginning April 17.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: Burchett signals support for sending US criminals to El Salvador
item.description: Rep. Tim Burchett (R-Tenn.) on Wednesday defended President Donald Trump’s suggestion that U.S. citizens convicted of violent crimes be sent to Salvadoran prisons.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Former MLS player Aaron Boupendza dies after falling from building in China, soccer officials say
item.description: The Gabonese soccer federation announced Wednesday that Aaron Boupendza, a striker for its men's national team, has died after falling from the 11th floor of a building in China.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Family holds funeral for young mother, only to learn she's still alive
item.description: A New York family is navigating an emotional journey after learning their loved one, whom officials declared dead last year, is actually alive and living out of state. Shanita Hopkins reached out to Nexstar's WROC with a very unusual situation.
item.dc:creator: Isabel Garcia, KTLA 5
